description: UX polish pass addressing the grocery list density on mobile and desktop, loading states, recipe title length enforcement, and minor theme inconsistencies. Based on full desktop + mobile video walkthrough.

userStories.title: Create skeleton UI component
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a reusable Skeleton component so that I can replace bare spinners with polished loading states throughout the app.
acceptanceCriteria: src/components/ui/skeleton.tsx exists and exports a Skeleton component
Skeleton renders a div with animate-pulse rounded-md bg-muted classes
Skeleton accepts className and other div props
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Standard shadcn/ui skeleton pattern. Use cn() from '@/lib/utils' for className merging. This is a prerequisite for US-002. File should be minimal — ~10 lines.

userStories.title: Replace bare spinners with skeleton loading states
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see meaningful skeleton layouts while pages load instead of a blank white screen with a tiny spinner.
acceptanceCriteria: RecipeClubEvents.tsx: bare spinner replaced with 3 skeleton event card placeholders matching event card shape (avatar circle, title bar, date/time bars, action buttons bar)
MealPlanPage.tsx: bare spinner in meal plan view replaced with skeleton week grid (7 column headers + placeholder cells)
EventDetailPage.tsx: bare spinner replaced with skeleton header card + Recipes/Groceries/Pantry tab skeleton
PersonalMealDetailPage.tsx: bare spinner replaced with skeleton header card + tab skeleton
RecipeHub.tsx: bare spinner replaced with skeleton recipe card placeholders (3 cards matching recipe card shape: title bar, author bar, action row, tag bar)
All skeletons use the Skeleton component from US-001
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Spinner locations: RecipeClubEvents.tsx ~line 208 (animate-spin div inside flex py-12). MealPlanPage.tsx ~line 427 (animate-spin div). EventDetailPage.tsx — search for animate-spin in the main loading return. PersonalMealDetailPage.tsx — search for animate-spin in the main loading return. RecipeHub.tsx ~line 688 (animate-spin div inside flex py-12). Each skeleton should preserve the page background gradient and header so the page doesn't flash. Keep skeletons simple: a few rounded rectangles of appropriate widths/heights. Import Skeleton from '@/components/ui/skeleton'.

userStories.title: Increase grocery row padding and category spacing
userStories.description: As a user, I want grocery list items to have more breathing room so the list is easier to read and scan.
acceptanceCriteria: GroceryItemRow.tsx: outer div class changes from 'py-px px-2' to 'py-1.5 px-2'
GroceryCategoryGroup.tsx: outer wrapper div class changes from 'mb-2' to 'mb-5'
GroceryCategoryGroup.tsx: header row div class changes from 'mb-1' to 'mb-2'
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: GroceryItemRow.tsx line 154: the outer div with 'flex items-center justify-between py-px px-2 hover:bg-gray-50 rounded group'. GroceryCategoryGroup.tsx line 26: outer wrapper div with 'mb-2'. Line 27: header div with 'flex items-center gap-2 mb-1'. CSS-only changes, no logic changes needed.

userStories.title: Replace recipe name badges with color-coded dots in Combined view
userStories.description: As a user, I want recipe sources shown as small colored dots instead of full recipe name badges so the grocery list is much more compact, especially on mobile where long names wrap to multiple lines.
acceptanceCriteria: GroceryListSection.tsx defines RECIPE_COLORS array with 8 distinct Tailwind bg-* color classes
GroceryListSection.tsx builds recipeColorMap: Record<string, string> mapping each recipe name to a color from RECIPE_COLORS
GroceryListSection.tsx renders a color legend above the Combined tab items: flex-wrap row of dots + recipe names
recipeColorMap is passed through GroceryCategoryGroup to GroceryItemRow in the Combined tab only
GroceryCategoryGroup.tsx accepts and threads recipeColorMap?: Record<string, string> prop to GroceryItemRow
GroceryItemRow.tsx accepts recipeColorMap?: Record<string, string> prop
When recipeColorMap is provided, render colored dot spans instead of Badge components for each sourceRecipe
Each dot has a title={recipe} attribute for hover tooltip
Per-recipe tabs are unaffected (sourceRecipes: [] already means no badges render)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: GroceryListSection.tsx: RECIPE_COLORS could be ['bg-blue-400', 'bg-emerald-400', 'bg-amber-400', 'bg-rose-400', 'bg-violet-400', 'bg-cyan-400', 'bg-orange-400', 'bg-teal-400']. Build recipeColorMap by mapping recipesWithIngredients names + 'General' to colors. Legend placement: above the {Array.from(smartGrouped.entries()).map(...)} block inside TabsContent value='combined' (~line 299). Legend: <div className='flex flex-wrap gap-x-3 gap-y-1 mb-3 text-xs text-muted-foreground'>. Dot span: <span className={cn('inline-block h-2.5 w-2.5 rounded-full shrink-0', color)} title={recipe} />. GroceryCategoryGroup.tsx: add recipeColorMap prop, thread it to each GroceryItemRow. GroceryItemRow.tsx lines 173-181: when recipeColorMap provided, render dots instead of <Badge>.

userStories.title: Replace tab bar with Select dropdown on mobile
userStories.description: As a mobile user, I want to use a dropdown to switch between recipe tabs so I can access all tabs instead of only seeing 1-2 truncated tab names.
acceptanceCriteria: On mobile (below sm breakpoint), the TabsList is hidden and a Select dropdown appears instead
On desktop (sm and above), the original TabsList shows and the Select is hidden
The Select includes all tabs: Combined (if hasAnyIngredients), per-recipe tabs, General (if hasGeneralTab)
Selecting an option in the Select calls setActiveTab with the correct value
The Select shows the currently active tab as its selected value
Export buttons (GroceryExportMenu + Recombine) stack below the tab selector on mobile
On desktop, export buttons remain on the same row as the tab list (current behavior preserved)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes:
GroceryListSection.tsx lines 258-289: the tab header area. Import Select, SelectContent, SelectItem, SelectTrigger, SelectValue from '@/components/ui/select' (already exists). Wrap TabsList in
. Add
before it. Change outer container from 'flex items-center justify-between gap-2 mb-3' to 'flex flex-col sm:flex-row sm:items-center sm:justify-between gap-2 mb-3'.

userStories.title: Collapse checked items within grocery categories
userStories.description: As a user, I want checked items to collapse into a summary so I can focus on what I still need to buy.
acceptanceCriteria: GroceryCategoryGroup.tsx splits items into unchecked and checked arrays based on checkedItems Set
Unchecked items render normally
When checked.length > 0, a native <details>/<summary> element renders below unchecked items
Summary text shows '{n} item checked' or '{n} items checked' (correct pluralization)
Checked items inside <details> render at reduced opacity (opacity-50)
When checkedItems is undefined (non-interactive mode), all items render as before with no change
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes:
GroceryCategoryGroup.tsx: add const unchecked = checkedItems ? items.filter(i => !checkedItems.has(i.name)) : items and const checked = checkedItems ? items.filter(i => checkedItems.has(i.name)) : []. Render unchecked normally. Then: {checkedItems && checked.length > 0 && (
{checked.length} item{checked.length !== 1 ? 's' : ''} checked
{checked.map(...)}
)}. The existing GroceryItemRow renders in both lists unchanged.

userStories.title: Add recipe title max length and character counter
userStories.description: As a user creating a recipe, I want the title input to enforce a 50-character limit so recipe names don't break grocery tab layouts.
acceptanceCriteria: Recipe name Input in RecipeInputForm.tsx has maxLength={50}
A character count indicator appears below the Input showing '{n}/50'
Character count text is text-xs text-muted-foreground
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes:
RecipeInputForm.tsx ~line 138-143: the Input with id='recipe-input-name'. Add maxLength={50} prop. Add

{formData.name.length}/50

below the Input (or below the FormItem wrapping it). The form data field is formData.name.

userStories.title: Normalize background gradient opacity and fix Deposit pill colors
userStories.description: As a user, I want consistent visual styling across all pages with matching gradient strength and color palette.
acceptanceCriteria: Dashboard.tsx: background gradient classes change from '/40' opacity to '/30' (from-purple-light/40 via-white to-orange-light/40 → /30)
src/pages/Index.tsx: background gradient changes from full-strength 'from-purple-light via-white to-orange-light' to '/30' variants
src/pages/NotFound.tsx: background gradient changes from full-strength to '/30' variants
Dashboard.tsx: Deposit stats pill changes from 'bg-green-50 border-green-300 text-green-600' to 'bg-green/5 border-green/20 text-green-600'
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Dashboard.tsx: search for 'from-purple-light/40' and 'to-orange-light/40' — change to /30. Also search for 'bg-green-50' in the stats pills section — the Deposit pill uses default Tailwind green tokens; change bg-green-50 to bg-green/5 and border-green-300 to border-green/20 (text-green-600 can stay or change to text-green). Index.tsx: search for 'from-purple-light via-white to-orange-light' — add /30 to each color stop. NotFound.tsx: same pattern. These are purely CSS class string changes.
